name: Almendrales Forest Park Loop
name_original: El Pastor – circular por el Pinar de los Almendrales
description: Jog the moderate 2.6-mile El Pastor circular route in Pinar de los Almendrales, featuring pine forests, panoramic views, and 700 feet of gai
region_intro: Jogging routes around La Axarquía traverse a diverse landscape, blending mountains, valleys, cliffs, and coastlines. The region features extensive natural parks like Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama, offering routes through dramatic hills and verdant forests. Joggers can also explore paths along rivers, through agricultural terraces with olive and almond trees, and connect charming white villages. This varied terrain provides a dynamic backdrop for running, from challenging mountain trails to accessible coastal paths. Best jogging routes around La Axarquía The most popular jogging route is El Saltillo Trail, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 58 minutes to complete. This route features impressive suspension bridges and wooden footbridges along a ravine. Another top favourite among local runners is Natural Park Montes de Málaga – running circular by Los Torrijos, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This route offers a scenic run through the natural park, known for its botanical diversity. Local runners also love the Embalse El Limonero – circular by the La Concepción Forest Park, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail leading through a forest park and around a reservoir, often completed in about 32 minutes. Jogging around La Axarquía is defined by diverse landscapes including natural parks, coastal paths, and white villages. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy forest loops to moderate mountain trails. The routes in La Axarquía are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 570 reviews. More than 44000 runners have used komoot to explore La Axarquía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in La Axarquía?
A: La Axarquía offers a vast network of over 600 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These trails traverse diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to mountain trails within natural parks.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or families in La Axarquía?
A: Yes, La Axarquía has numerous easy jogging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996222837/embalse-el-limonero-circular-by-the-la-concepcion-forest-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse El Limonero – circular by the La Concepción Forest Park&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.0-mile (4.8 km) trail through a forest park and around a reservoir. The region's coastal paths, like sections of the Málaga Coastal Path, also offer gentle, flat options perfect for a relaxed run.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see while jogging in La Axarquía?
A: La Axarquía's routes offer stunning natural attractions. You can encounter dramatic hills and verdant forests in the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996268636/el-saltillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Trail&lt;/a&gt; features an impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;suspension bridge&lt;/a&gt; and wooden footbridges over a ravine. The Río Chillar Trail, though sometimes requiring navigation through water, is known for its picturesque gorges, refreshing waterfalls, and natural pools. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1084101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Chillar riverbed&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3912340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Higuerón River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the region?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in La Axarquía are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996225864/natural-park-montes-de-malaga-running-circular-by-los-torrijos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park Montes de Málaga – running circular by Los Torrijos&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.4-mile (8.7 km) path known for its botanical diversity. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996222837/embalse-el-limonero-circular-by-the-la-concepcion-forest-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse El Limonero – circular by the La Concepción Forest Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in La Axarquía?
A: The running routes in La Axarquía are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 570 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain paths to serene coastal jogs, and the opportunity to explore charming white villages and natural parks.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through or near historical sites and white villages?
A: Yes, La Axarquía is famous for its enchanting white villages of Moorish origin, such as Cómpeta, Frigiliana, and Canillas de Aceituno. Many trails connect these historic settlements, allowing you to combine your run with cultural exploration. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7262649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba of Málaga and the Roman Theatre&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7114385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba Fortress&lt;/a&gt; near some routes.

Q: Are there shaded routes for jogging during warmer months?
A: During warmer months, seeking shaded routes is advisable. La Axarquía features pine forests, especially near villages like Sedella and Canillas de Aceituno, which can offer some respite from the sun. Routes through natural parks, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996225864/natural-park-montes-de-malaga-running-circular-by-los-torrijos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park Montes de Málaga – running circular by Los Torrijos&lt;/a&gt;, often provide tree cover. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996222837/embalse-el-limonero-circular-by-the-la-concepcion-forest-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse El Limonero – circular by the La Concepción Forest Park&lt;/a&gt; is another good option with forest sections.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of running trails in La Axarquía?
A: The running trails in La Axarquía offer a wide range of difficulty levels. You'll find easy forest loops and coastal paths, moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996268636/el-saltillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, and more challenging mountain routes, including ascents towards La Maroma, the highest peak in Malaga province. Out of over 600 routes, approximately 50 are easy, 350 are moderate, and over 200 are difficult.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, many routes in La Axarquía provide spectacular panoramic views. Trails leading to and around La Maroma, the highest peak, offer breathtaking vistas of the surrounding mountains, the Sierra Nevada, and the Viñuela reservoir. The El Fuerte Trail near Frigiliana is also known for its rewarding 360-degree views from El Fuerte Peak.

Q: Can I find routes with amenities like cafes or recreation areas nearby?
A: Some running areas in La Axarquía are close to amenities. For example, the El Alcázar Recreational Area in Alcaucín serves as a gateway to trails and features a botanical route. The Lagar de Torrijos Ecomuseum and Recreation Area also offers easily accessible paths. You might also find cafes in the charming white villages that many routes connect, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Terrazita Café at Gibralfaro Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some unique or less-known running experiences in La Axarquía?
A: Beyond the well-trodden paths, La Axarquía offers unique running experiences. The Río Chillar Trail near Nerja provides an adventurous run along a riverbed, navigating gorges and natural pools. The recently renovated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996268636/el-saltillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, part of the Great Málaga Path, is recognized as one of Spain's most beautiful certified hiking trails, offering a thrilling experience with its suspension bridge and footbridges.

Q: Are there routes suitable for running with dogs?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations vary by trail and local regulations, many natural trails and paths in La Axarquía's rural areas and natural parks are generally suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept under control. It's always recommended to check local signage and respect wildlife. The varied terrain, from forests to open agricultural lands, offers plenty of space for you and your canine companion.
tips: Thyme covered hillsides, fragrant pine forests, fruiting olive trees and eagles soaring overhead — this truly is a magical place to explore. Whatsmore, the panoramic view from this spot is epic. You can see Malaga, the mountains, the Mediterranean, little villages, lavish country escapes and intriguing ruins. The trails in this neck of the woods are very testing; steep, rocky, dusty and loose. But that’s all part of the fun.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the El Pastor circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 4.26 kilometers long and is designed for jogging. On average, it takes about 35-40 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is the El Pastor circular trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While not excessively long, it features varied and challenging terrain, including sections that can be steep, rocky, dusty, and loose. It requires a good level of fitness, so beginners might find it quite demanding.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the El Pastor circular route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a varied and challenging experience. The terrain includes sections of singletrack and paths that can be steep, rocky, dusty, and loose. It's a testing route that offers both ascents and descents through natural landscapes.

faqs.question: What can I see along the El Pastor circular route?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful natural surroundings and extensive views. You'll jog through fragrant pine forests, past hillsides covered in thyme, and olive trees. A notable highlight is the El Pastor viewpoint, which provides panoramic vistas of Málaga, the mountains, the Mediterranean Sea, small villages, and intriguing ruins. Keep an eye out for eagles soaring overhead!

faqs.question: Does the El Pastor circular trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Sendero del Mirador and the Camino de los Almendrales.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the El Pastor circular trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the trail is located in the Pinar de los Almendrales area. We recommend checking local maps or community forums for the most convenient and accessible parking options near the trailhead before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the El Pastor circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in natural parks like Pinar de los Almendrales are dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route is typically during the cooler months, such as spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November). During these seasons, the temperatures are more pleasant, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making the challenging terrain even more demanding.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the Pinar de los Almendrales?
faqs.answer: Access to the Pinar de los Almendrales, where this trail is located, generally does not require specific permits or entrance fees for recreational use. However, always respect local regulations and signage within the natural area.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the Pinar de los Almendrales trailheads?
faqs.answer: The Pinar de los Almendrales is a natural area, so amenities like cafes and restaurants are not directly on the trail. You will likely find options in nearby villages or in Málaga, which is visible from the trail. It's a good idea to bring your own water and snacks for the duration of your jog.

faqs.question: Is the El Pastor circular route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating and challenging terrain, including steep and rocky sections, this route might be too demanding for younger children or those not accustomed to strenuous jogging. It's best suited for families with older, experienced, and fit children.
